Makefile: Verilator + UVM_HOME compile RTL, interface, and test_*.sv UVM phases: build → connect → run_test → report (same pattern as Module 2) Sequence → driver → DUT → monitor → scoreboard (read SCOREBOARD at end) Typical command: make SIM=verilator TEST=test_* (see module6 demo slide) Loopback / hooks connect... |

| 23 | 1. Multi-agent coordination strategy | 0:38 | 1. Multi-agent coordination strategy. Assign each agent a virtual interface and address/data range via ConfigDB Run parallel sequences with objections on env or test (not per short sequence) Protocol checker runs passively — does not drive, only flags violations Use predictable directed traffic before enabling constrained-random stress Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 24 | 2. System-level closure workflow (1/2) | 0:38 | 2. System-level closure workflow (1/2). Scoreboard empty at end — no pending expected transactions Protocol checker reports zero violations Regression: ./scripts/module6.sh --all-examples --uvm-tests Debug order: signal wave → monitor log → scoreboard mismatch → checker message Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 25 | 2. System-level closure workflow (2/2) | 0:16 | 2. System-level closure workflow (2/2). Performance: --jobs N for parallel example builds during development |

| 47 | Detail: Protocol Handshake Patterns (2) | 0:20 | Protocol and design detail: Protocol Handshake Patterns (2). Purpose: AXI read transaction Key: Drive address, wait for ready, capture data From MODULE6 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |
| 48 | Detail: Bounded Wait Checking (1) | 0:28 | Protocol and design detail: Bounded Wait Checking (1). Purpose: Check protocol doesn't wait indefinitely Key: Count cycles, report if exceeded bound Purpose: Ignore protocol during reset Key: Use continue to skip reset cycles From MODULE6 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |
| 49 | Detail: Bounded Wait Checking (2) | 0:20 | Protocol and design detail: Bounded Wait Checking (2). Purpose: Continuously monitor protocol Key: Forever loop with clock synchronization From MODULE6 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |
| 50 | Detail: Associative Array Operations (1) | 0:28 | Protocol and design detail: Associative Array Operations (1). Purpose: Store transactions by sequence number Key: Use sequence number as index Purpose: Check if transaction exists Key: Use exists() method From MODULE6 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |
| 51 | Detail: Associative Array Operations (2) | 0:20 | Protocol and design detail: Associative Array Operations (2). Purpose: Remove matched transactions Key: Use delete() method From MODULE6 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |
| 52 | Detail: Transaction Matching (1) | 0:28 | Protocol and design detail: Transaction Matching (1). Purpose: Match transactions across streams Key: Check both streams, compare, clean up Purpose: Connect multiple streams to one scoreboard Key: One scoreboard can receive from multiple sources From MODULE6 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |

Return Type: bit (1 = success, 0 = failure) Purpose: Generate random test vectors with constraints Usage: Call after creating transaction, before sending to sequencer Key: void'() suppresses Verilator warning about ignored return value Constraints: Use with { constraint_expression; } for conditional randomization |
| 60 | 1. Testbench Architecture (6/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (6/13). Method Signature: task body(); (no parameters, no return) Purpose: Generate and send multiple transactions to sequencer Execution Flow: Key: repeat loop generates multiple transactions sequentially Blocking Behavior: start_item() and finish_item() both block |
| 61 | 1. Testbench Architecture (7/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (7/13). Method Signature: function void build_phase(uvm_phase phase); Phase Type: Build-time phase (function, executes top-down) Purpose: Create all child components of the agent Execution Order: Key: All components created in agent's build_phase(), before connect_phase() |
| 62 | 1. Testbench Architecture (8/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (8/13). | 63 | 1. Testbench Architecture (9/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (9/13). TLM Pattern: Port (initiator) connects to Export (target) Transaction Flow: Sequence → Sequencer (export) → Driver (port) → DUT Connection Timing: Must happen after build_phase() (components must exist) Method Signature: task run_phase(uvm_phase phase); Phase Type: Run-time phase (task, executes concurrently) |
| 64 | 1. Testbench Architecture (10/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (10/13). Execution Flow: Key: Objection mechanism controls phase completion Sequence Execution: seq.start(sequencer) blocks until body() completes Transaction Flow: Test → Sequence → Sequencer → Driver → DUT Timing: All run_phase tasks execute concurrently (forked) |
| 65 | 1. Testbench Architecture (11/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (11/13). Separation of concerns (driver, monitor, sequencer) improves reusability Component factory and configuration provide flexibility Phase-based execution model ensures proper initialization and execution Transaction-based communication decouples components Component hierarchy: Test → Env → Agent → Sequencer/Driver/Monitor |
| 66 | 1. Testbench Architecture (12/13) | 0:36 | 1. Testbench Architecture (12/13). Phase execution: Build → Connect → Run → Report Sequence execution: seq.start(sequencer) in run_phase UVM component hierarchy construction Transaction generation and driving Component phase execution |

| 107 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (11/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (11/21). Usage: Before creating agent Key: Set config, then create component Purpose: Retrieve agent configuration Usage: In agent's build_phase() Key: Check return value, provide defaults |
| 108 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (12/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (12/21). Usage: Alternative to start_item()/finish_item() Key: Useful in virtual sequences for direct execution Note: Transaction must be created and randomized first Purpose: Coordinate multiple sequencers Key: Set sequencer references before starting |
| 109 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (13/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (13/21). Usage: In driver/monitor build_phase() Key: Must be set before component can access signals Purpose: Set interface for all components Usage: In module before run_test() Key: Use wildcard "*" to match all components |
| 110 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (14/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (14/21). Key: Assert VALID, wait for READY, deassert VALID Purpose: AXI write transaction Key: Drive address and data, wait for both ready Purpose: AXI read transaction Key: Drive address, wait for ready, capture data |
| 111 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (15/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (15/21). Key: Count cycles, report if exceeded bound Purpose: Ignore protocol during reset Key: Use continue to skip reset cycles Purpose: Continuously monitor protocol Key: Forever loop with clock synchronization |
| 112 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (16/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (16/21). Key: Use sequence number as index Purpose: Check if transaction exists Key: Use exists() method Purpose: Remove matched transactions Key: Use delete() method |
| 113 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (17/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (17/21). Key: Check both streams, compare, clean up Purpose: Connect multiple streams to one scoreboard Key: One scoreboard can receive from multiple sources Problem: uvm_fatal("NO_VIF", ...) error Solution: Set interface in module before run_test() |
| 114 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (18/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (18/21). Problem: Agent uses default values Solution: Check return value, provide defaults Code: if (!uvm_config_db#(int)::get(...)) agent_id = 0; Problem: Null pointer exception Solution: Set sequencer references before starting |
| 115 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (19/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (19/21). Problem: Simulation hangs waiting for READY Solution: Use bounded wait in protocol checker Code: while (condition && cycles < max_cycles) Problem: Unmatched transactions accumulate Solution: Delete matched transactions |
| 116 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (20/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (20/21). Check: Virtual interface retrieved in build_phase() Check: Interface set before run_test() Check: Clock running in module Check: Sequencer references set before start() Check: Objection raised in test's run_phase() |
| 117 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (21/21) | 0:36 | 5. Multi-Channel Scoreboards (21/21). Check: Sequence numbers assigned correctly Check: Both streams connected to scoreboard Check: write() method called for both streams Check: Reset handling (skip during reset) Check: Bounded wait threshold appropriate |

| 126 | Example 1: Testbench Architecture | 0:32 | Example 1: Testbench Architecture. Transaction Class (ArchTxn): Base transaction with address, data, and write/read control Sequencer (ArchSequencer): UVM sequencer for transaction flow control Driver (ArchDriver): UVM driver that receives and drives transactions Monitor (ArchMonitor): UVM monitor that observes DUT behavior Agent (ArchAgent): UVM agent containing sequencer, driver, and monitor... |

Transaction (MA_Txn): Transaction with agent ID and data fields Agent (MA_Agent): Parameterized agent with configurable agent ID Virtual Sequence (MA_VirtualSeq): Coordinates sequences across multiple sequencers Environment (MA_Env): Contains two agent instances (a0 and a1) Test (MultiAgentTest): Orchestrates multi-agent verification... |
| 129 | Demo: Multi-Agent Environment | 0:45 | Demo: Multi-Agent Environment. Watch the terminal output and confirm you see the expected pass message. |
| 130 | Example 3: Protocol Verification | 0:32 | Example 3: Protocol Verification. Interface (axi_lite_if): AXI4-Lite interface with all required signals Transaction (AxiTxn): AXI transaction with read/write, address, and data Driver (AxiDriver): Protocol-aware driver implementing AXI4-Lite handshake protocol Monitor (AxiMonitor): Protocol monitor for observing AXI transactions Environment (ProtocolEnv): Protocol verification environment... |
| 131 | Demo: Protocol Verification | 0:45 | Demo: Protocol Verification. Watch the terminal output and confirm you see the expected pass message. |

Interface (chk_if): Simple handshake interface with valid/ready signals Checker Component (CheckerComp): UVM component that monitors protocol rules Protocol Rules: Valid must stay asserted until ready (bounded wait) Test (ProtocolCheckersTest): Test that exercises protocol checker module6/examples/protocol_checkers/README.md |

Transaction (SB_Txn): Scoreboard transaction with stream ID, sequence number, and data Stream Producer (StreamProducer): Component that generates transactions for a stream Multi-Channel Scoreboard (MultiChannelScoreboard): Scoreboard that matches transactions from multiple streams Test (ScoreboardsTest): Test with two stream producers and a scoreboard... |

| 137 | Virtual Interface Not Set | 0:24 | Virtual Interface Not Set. Problem: uvm_fatal("NO_VIF", ...) error Solution: Set interface in module before run_test() Code: uvm_config_db#(virtual if)::set(null, "*", "vif", vif); |
| 138 | Agent Configuration Not Retrieved | 0:24 | Agent Configuration Not Retrieved. Problem: Agent uses default values Solution: Check return value, provide defaults Code: if (!uvm_config_db#(int)::get(...)) agent_id = 0; |
| 139 | Virtual Sequence References Not Set | 0:24 | Virtual Sequence References Not Set. Problem: Null pointer exception Solution: Set sequencer references before starting Code: vseq.seqr0 = env.a0.seqr; vseq.start(null); |
| 140 | Protocol Handshake Deadlock | 0:24 | Protocol Handshake Deadlock. Problem: Simulation hangs waiting for READY Solution: Use bounded wait in protocol checker Code: while (condition && cycles < max_cycles) |
| 141 | Scoreboard Memory Leak | 0:28 | Scoreboard Memory Leak. Problem: Unmatched transactions accumulate Solution: Delete matched transactions Code: q0.delete(t.seq); q1.delete(t.seq); Check: Virtual interface retrieved in build_phase() |
| 142 | Testbench Architecture | 0:28 | Testbench Architecture. Design a hierarchical testbench with multiple agents Implement component separation of concerns Create reusable agent and environment patterns Structure testbench for maintainability |

Design complex testbench architectures with hierarchical component organization Coordinate multiple agents using virtual sequences Verify protocols using protocol-shaped drivers and monitors Implement protocol checkers for compliance verification Build multi-channel scoreboards for transaction matching From MODULE6 Learning Outcomes. |

        ## Section narration (edit for TTS)

        - **Design architecture (Multi-agent testbench architecture, Protocol verification architecture, Module 6 example map):** Walk through the block diagram, then relate each block to files under module6/examples/.
- **Execution:** Explain make run / UVM make steps, then walk the artifact table and directed-test sequence slide by slide.
- **Verification (Multi-agent coordination strategy, System-level closure workflow):** Explain what stimulus is applied, what is checked, and what is intentionally out of scope.
- **Syllabus:** Cover 5 topic section(s) — pause on protocol timing and signals.
- **Before exercises:** Ask learners to recall the learning outcomes slide; they should explain each bullet in their own words.
- **Hands-on:** Run module6/EXAMPLES.md labs; narrate expected PASS lines.

        ## Notes

        - Slides from **Before You Start**, **Design Architecture**, **Verification & Testing Methods**, **Topics Covered**, **EXAMPLES.md**, and **Learning Outcomes**.
        - Full detail: `docs/MODULE6.md` and `module6/EXAMPLES.md`.
        - Regenerate: `regenerate_course_outlines.sh <course_root> --module 6`
